content.problemStatement:
Given a positive integer NNN, find sum of its digits.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single integer NNN.


Output Format

Output on a single line: the sum of digits of NNN.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a separate line the sum of the decimal digits of N.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Digit sum of 2 is 2; 123456789 has digits 1 through 9, sum=45

solution.approach: The digit sum of a number can be obtained by repeatedly extracting its last digit using the modulo operator (n % 10) and adding it to a running total, then removing the last digit with integer division (n /= 10). This process is performed for each test case independently. The algorithm runs in O(D) time per test case, where D is the number of digits of N (at most 10 for the given constraints), and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of digits) ≈ O(T * log10(N))

code.Python3:
import sys

def digit_sum(n):
    s=0
    while n:
        s+= n%10
        n//=10
    return s

data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T=int(data[0])
out=[]
idx=1
for _ in range(T):
    n=int(data[idx]); idx+=1
    out.append(str(digit_sum(n)))
print('\n'.join(out))
